hydross.SCORECARD_FINDING: 28th percentile; HPS (558) ran at 0.38x the sample average, and overheal (75.9%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (58.4%) by 17.5 points - the widest overheal gap of the raid. One death this kill.
hydross.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing (17.1%) and Flash Heal (19.2%) both undershot the sample's shares (30.4%, 32.6%); Prayer of Mending (32.0%) ran well ahead of the sample's 14.0% to compensate, the single largest composition gap of the raid.
hydross.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Mana Potion went unused against a 59% sample usage rate. Shadowfiend and Chakra both landed in line, self-targeted. The one death this kill (Hezrun) trails the Shadowfiend cast by 18 seconds - too far, and Shadowfiend restores the caster's own mana rather than protecting another player, so this doesn't read as a missed save.
hydross.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (90.0%) ran well above the sample average (71.0%) - the widest coverage gap of the raid; top-target concentration (39.6%, Luffydonoo) landed in line with the sample.

lurker.SCORECARD_FINDING: 49th percentile; HPS (895) ran at 0.38x the sample average, and overheal (47.2%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (40.5%) by 6.7 points. One death this kill.
lurker.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing (41.4%) undershot the sample's dominant 71.2% share by the widest margin of the raid; Flash Heal (21.5%) and Renew (20.9%) both ran well ahead of the sample to make up the difference.
lurker.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Mana Potion went unused against a 73% sample usage rate. Shadowfiend and Chakra both landed in line, self-targeted. The one death this kill (Staubaoz) trails the Shadowfiend cast by 58 seconds, too far to read as related.
lurker.TARGET_FINDING: The most tank-concentrated kill so far: Luffydonoo took 35.4% of healing, nearly triple the sample's typical top-target share (11.9%), with coverage (67.7%) also well above the sample average (45.7%).

leotheras.SCORECARD_FINDING: 70th percentile, one of the strongest kills of the raid; overheal (49.7%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (38.4%) by 11.3 points, and HPM (4.86) ran at 0.49x the sample average. Five deaths this kill, the second-highest death toll of the raid.
leotheras.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing (51.8%) undershot the sample's 77.3% share; Flash Heal (20.1%) ran well ahead of the sample's 2.9% to help compensate.
leotheras.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Mana Potion went unused against an 81% sample usage rate. The five deaths split into two clusters, the closest trailing the single Shadowfiend cast by 3.4 and 6.7 seconds - tight timing, but Shadowfiend restores the caster's own mana rather than protecting another player, so even this close pair doesn't read as a real missed save.
leotheras.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (48.8%) and top-target concentration (15.7%, Captinhelp) both landed in line with the sample.

karathress.SCORECARD_FINDING: 68th percentile; HPM (5.24, 0.67x the sample average) was the most efficient ratio of the raid, and overheal (42.7%) landed just under the sample's worst parse. No deaths this kill.
karathress.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing (27.5%) undershot the sample's 63.5% share by the widest composition gap of the raid; Renew (24.9%) and Flash Heal (26.2%) both ran well ahead of the sample instead.
karathress.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Shadowfiend, Chakra, and Mana Potion all landed in line with the sample, all self-targeted. No deaths this kill, so no cooldown-timing question to check.
karathress.TARGET_FINDING: The most tank-concentrated kill of the raid: Thugalina took 39.2% of healing, well above the sample's typical top-target share (16.4%), with coverage (76.7%) also the widest coverage gap of the raid (+29.3 points over the 47.4% sample average).

morogrim.SCORECARD_FINDING: The strongest kill of the raid by percentile (72nd); overheal (54.3%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (45.4%) by 8.9 points, and HPM (4.72) ran at 0.65x the sample average. No deaths this kill.
morogrim.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing (46.8%) undershot the sample's 58.4% share, one of the smaller composition gaps of the raid; Renew (20.0%) ran ahead of the sample's 8.7% to help compensate.
morogrim.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Mana Potion went unused against a 74% sample usage rate; Shadowfiend and Chakra both landed in line, self-targeted. No deaths this kill, so no cooldown-timing question to check.
morogrim.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (56.0%) and top-target concentration (32.1%, Captinhelp) both ran well above the sample averages (44.0%, 17.0%) - a real, tank-heavy kill.

vashj.SCORECARD_FINDING: 43rd percentile; HPM (3.88) ran at 0.61x the sample average, and overheal (54.8%) sat comfortably below this boss's unusually high sample worst-parse ceiling (74.6%). Four deaths this kill.
vashj.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing (35.5%) actually exceeded the sample's 29.8% share, the only kill this raid where it did - the smallest real composition gap of the raid.
vashj.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Shadowfiend, Chakra, and Mana Potion all landed in line with the sample, all self-targeted. Of the four deaths this kill, the closest (Calamety) trails a Chakra cast by just 1.1 seconds - tight timing, but Chakra is a self-targeted cooldown rather than a save for another player, so it doesn't read as a real missed opportunity.
vashj.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (58.5%) ran above the sample average (41.4%); top-target concentration (25.5%, Captinhelp) also ran above the sample average (13.1%).

alar.SCORECARD_FINDING: 32nd percentile; overheal (65.9%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (46.7%) by 19.2 points, one of the widest gaps of the raid, and HPM (2.94) ran at 0.5x the sample average. One death this kill.
alar.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing (16.2%) undershot the sample's 42.6% share; Renew (36.2%) and Prayer of Mending (19.6%) both ran well ahead of the sample instead.
alar.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Mana Potion was cast here (unlike four other kills this raid) and landed in line with the sample's 96% usage rate; Shadowfiend and Chakra also landed in line. The one death this kill (Leechers) trails the Shadowfiend cast by 88.5 seconds, far too distant to read as related.
alar.TARGET_FINDING: Thugalina took the largest healing share (36.7%), well above the sample's typical top-target concentration (18.9%), with coverage (73.0%) also running well above the sample average (49.8%).

voidreaver.SCORECARD_FINDING: 39th percentile; HPS (2183, 0.58x the sample average) and target coverage (41.3% vs. a 37.7% sample average) were both close to sample-normal - the smallest target-spread gap of the raid. Overheal (38.2%) exceeded this boss's tight worst-parse ceiling (23.5%) by 14.7 points despite being the lowest absolute overheal number of the raid. No deaths this kill.
voidreaver.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing (79.1%) ran close to the sample's 81.9% share, the closest composition match of the raid; the largest listed gap is Prayer of Healing, a spell never cast here that the sample uses on 6.0% of parses.
voidreaver.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Shadowfiend, Chakra, and Mana Potion all landed in line with the sample, all self-targeted. No deaths this kill, so no cooldown-timing question to check.
voidreaver.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (41.3%) and top-target concentration (13.9%, Captinhelp) both landed close to the sample average - the most evenly-spread kill of the raid.

solarian.SCORECARD_FINDING: 25th percentile; overheal (72.0%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (62.2%) by 9.8 points, and HPM (2.7) ran at 0.45x the sample average, the lowest efficiency ratio of the raid. No deaths this kill.
solarian.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Flash Heal (31.8%) undershot the sample's 42.1% share, the largest gap this kill; Renew (13.4%) ran well ahead of the sample's 2.6% to compensate, the largest gap in the character's favor this kill.
solarian.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Shadowfiend went unused against a 54% sample usage rate - the only kill this raid where Shadowfiend wasn't cast. Chakra and Mana Potion both landed in line. No deaths this kill, so no cooldown-timing question to check.
solarian.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (55.8%) and top-target concentration (15.4%, Leechers) both landed in line with the sample - the low HPS/HPM/overheal numbers above are the real story on this kill, not target spread.

kaelthas.SCORECARD_FINDING: The lowest percentile of the raid (19th); HPS (541) ran at 0.45x the sample average, and overheal (60.7%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (52.3%) by 8.4 points. By far the deadliest kill of the raid - 16 deaths, including one of Lasmur's own.
kaelthas.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing (27.5%) undershot the sample's 44.6% share; Renew (24.0%) and Prayer of Mending (17.2%) both ran ahead of the sample to help make up the difference.
kaelthas.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Fear Ward went unused against an 87% sample usage rate - the only cooldown gap flagged this kill. Shadowfiend, Chakra, and Mana Potion all landed in line, all self-targeted. With 16 deaths this kill, most trail the single Mana Potion cast by roughly 6-60 seconds; Lasmur's own death sits 23.4 seconds before it. Mana Potion restores the caster's own mana rather than protecting another player, so none of these - including Lasmur's own - read as a real missed save.
kaelthas.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (66.0%) ran well above the sample average (42.5%); top-target concentration (24.6%, Thugalina) also ran above the sample's typical 13.4%.

RaidOverview.GEAR_CONSISTENCY_FINDING: consistent except for a missing temporary weapon enchant on two kills
RaidOverview.GEAR_FINDING_NOTE: The Main Hand weapon (item 30108) and its permanent enchant were identical across all 10 kills; only the temporary weapon enchant was missing on Morogrim and Solarian specifically. All 9 enchantable slots carry a permanent enchant, and every kill had a complete consumable setup.
RaidOverview.RAID_SUMMARY_FINDING: A full 10-boss clear (all kills, no wipes) spanning the 19th to 72nd percentile. HPS ran below the Top 100 average on every kill (0.34x-0.67x), and overheal exceeded the sample's worst recorded parse on 8 of 10 kills - only Fathom-Lord Karathress (just under the worst-parse line) and Lady Vashj (a boss with an unusually high worst-parse ceiling) stayed in line. Circle of Healing consistently undershot the benchmark's share of the healing mix, closest on Void Reaver (79.1% vs. 81.9%) and actually exceeding it on Lady Vashj (35.5% vs. 29.8%); Renew and Prayer of Mending ran ahead of the sample on most kills to help make up the difference. Mana Potion went uncast on four kills (Hydross, Lurker, Leotheras, Morogrim) against sample usage rates of 59-81%. Kael'thas Sunstrider was both the weakest kill by percentile (19th) and by far the deadliest of the raid - 16 deaths, including one of Lasmur's own - while Morogrim Tidewalker was the strongest kill (72nd) and one of four kills with zero deaths (alongside Fathom-Lord Karathress, Void Reaver, and High Astromancer Solarian). No cooldown-timing correlation this raid points to a real missed save - Shadowfiend, Chakra, and Mana Potion are all self-targeted resource or buff tools, not defensive cooldowns for another player, even on Lady Vashj where one death landed just 1.1 seconds after a Chakra cast. Blessing of Life was never cast this raid night (0 of 10 kills). Gear was effectively consistent - the same Main Hand weapon and permanent enchant on all 10 kills, with only its temporary weapon enchant missing on two of them (Morogrim, Solarian) - and every kill had a complete consumable setup.
RaidOverview.RAID_WARNING_BANNER: Kael'thas Sunstrider was this raid's deadliest kill by a wide margin - 16 deaths, including one of Lasmur's own - and also the weakest kill by percentile (19th). Nothing in the nearby cooldown timing points to a real missed save anywhere this raid, since every cooldown cast near a death (Shadowfiend, Chakra, Mana Potion) is self-targeted rather than a defensive tool for another player.
